original: First recorded occurrence of a comet hitting the Sun releases energy equal to one million hydrogen bombs
13,000 meteors are seen in one hour near the Andromeda Galaxy, the closest major galaxy to the Milky Way at 2.537 million light-years from Earth
Guion Bluford becomes the first African American astronaut in space
Yves Pol of France runs a marathon backward in 3:57:57
Mike Powell of the US sets a long jump record of 29 ft 4½ in (8.95 m)
Street Fighter, the first arcade fighting game to introduce special attacks, designed byTakashi Nishiyama, is released

Maurice R. Hilleman, American microbiologist who developed over 36 vaccines including measles, mumps, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, chickenpox, meningitis, pneumonia, born in Miles City, Montana (d. 2005)
Algeria becomes the last country to stop selling leaded petrol, ending 99 years of gasoline use worldwide, saving 1.2 million lives a year

The MacLean brothers, Ewan (32), Jamie (31), and Lachlan (27) of Scotland arrive in Cairns, Australia in a rowboat from Lima, Peru, setting new record for non-stop unsupported crossing of the Pacific Ocean (139 days, 5 hours, 52 minutes)
Second interstellar comet detected by Ukrainian amateur astronomer Gennady Borisov, later named 2I/Borisov
NASA spacecraft Voyager 2 crosses the termination shock, where solar and interstellar winds meet (following Voyager 1 in 2004)

Burgess Shale fossil site, one of the most diverse and best-preserved in the world, is discovered by Charles Doolittle Walcott in the Canadian Rocky Mountains (now British Columbia's Yoho National Park)
Stefka Kostadinova of Bulgaria sets the women's high jump record at 2.09 m (6 ft 10½ in), one of the longest-standing records in athletics
Ben Johnson of Canada runs the 100 m in a world record time of 9.83 seconds
John Mauchly, American physicist who with J. Presper Eckert, designed the first general purpose electronic digital computer (ENIAC), born in Cincinnati, Ohio (d. 1980)
Octavia E. Butlerwins her first Hugo award for Best Short Story with the science fiction story "Speech Sounds"
David Jewitt and Jane Luu discover the object "1992 QB1" 4.4 billion kilometers from the Sun

American women's 4 x 100m freestyle relay team of Shirley Babashoff, Jane Barkman, Jenny Kemp, and Sandy Neilson beats East Germany by just 0.36 seconds to win gold at the Munich Olympics with a world record time of 3:55.19
Australian teenage swimmerShane Gouldwins her second of three gold medals in a world record time of 4:19.04 in the 400 m freestyle at the Munich Olympics
Sylvia Earle, American marine biologist, born in Gibbstown, New Jersey
Nancy Wake, New Zealand-born World War II secret agent (Special Operations Executive), born in Wellington, New Zealand (d. 2011)
US Senate confirmsThurgood Marshallas the first African American justice to serve on the Supreme Court
John Leonard Swigert Jr., American astronaut (Apollo 13), born in Denver, Colorado (d. 1982)
Hurricane Irma forms near the Cape Verde Islands and goes on to become a Category 5 hurricane, killing at least 102
Laurent de Brunhoff, French-American writer and illustrator (Babar the Elephant), born in Paris, France (d. 2024)

Hotline communication link between the Pentagon in Washington, D.C., and the Kremlin in Moscow is installed. Often known as the "red telephone," no phones are ever used, relying instead on Teletype equipment, fax machines, and later, secure email.
Amy Sherald, American painter (portrait of Michelle Obama), born in Columbus, Georgia
Emily Bear, American composer, musician and singer-songwriter (The Unofficial Bridgerton Musical), born in Rockford, Illinois
Late authorTerry Pratchett's unfinished works are destroyed by a steamroller as per his instructions
The Beatles release the single "Hey Jude," their first record on their Apple label in the UK
8th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Challenger 3 launches for a 6-day excursion
12th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41D): Discovery 1 launches for a 6-day excursion

Three-day Texas International Pop Festival opens in Lewisville, Texas, with 125,000 attendees, featuring performers such as Chicago Transit Authority, Delaney and Bonnie and Friends, Grand Funk Railroad,Janis Joplin, B.B. King, Led Zeppelin, Nazz, Sam and Dave, Sly and the Family Stone, and Johnny Winter

Three-day second Annual Sky River Rock Festival opens in Tenino, Washington, with 25,000 attendees, featuring performers such as James Cotton, Country Joe and the Fish, Flying Burrito Brothers, Dan Hicks and His Hot Licks, Mississippi Fred McDowell,Steve MillerBand, and Sons of Champlin
Section of Allalin Glacier wipes out construction site at Mattmark Dam near Saas-Fee, Switzerland
Brazilian court blocks PresidentMichel Temerfrom abolishing Renca, which would open parts of the Amazon to mining
Austrian explorers Julius von Payer and Karl Weyprecht discover the archipelago of Franz Josef Land in the Arctic Ocean
Outlook for the Great Barrier Reef is downgraded to very poor according to official Australian report
Hurricane Idalia makes landfall as a Category 3 storm in Florida's Big Bend, killing at least two people
Hassan IIMosque opens in Casablanca, the second-largest mosque in the world
Barbara Mary Ansell, UK founder of paediatric rheumatology, born in Warwick, England (d. 2001)
Carrie Saxon Perry, 1st African-American mayor of a major US city (Hartford CT), born in Hartford, Connecticut (d. 2018)
Christopher Collins, American actor (True Identity), voice actor (Cobra Commander-G.I. Joe) and comedian, born in Orange, New Jersey (d. 1994)
Lake Pontchartrain Causeway opens in Louisiana, the longest continuous bridge in the world
Compact audio cassette is first introduced by Philips at the Radio Exhibition in Berlin, Germany
People of East Timor vote for independence from Indonesia in a referendum
William Pennleaves England to sail to the New World
WWII: US GeneralDouglas MacArthurlands in Japan
Japan conducts a test of the NAMC YS-11, its first aircraft since the war and its only successful commercial aircraft before or after the war
Beginning of the Battle of Lake Poyang; two Chinese rebel leaders, Chen Youliang and Zhu Yuanzhang, clash in one of the largest naval battles in history during the Mongol-led Yuan Dynasty
Phillies lead Giants 8-6 in the top of the 9th, fans in the bleachers try to distract the Giants, umpire forfeits the game to the Giants, later overruled
New York Yankee Atley Donald pitches a baseball at a record 94.7 mph (152 km/h)
Master Sgt. Gary Gordon, American Medal of Honor recipient, born in Lincoln, Maine (d. 1993)
Hong Kong is liberated from Japanese control
Launch of the first Dutch satellite, ANS, from Vandenberg
In Jackson, Mississippi, the city's largest water treatment plant fails, leaving 150,000 people without safe running water and closing schools and businesses
Detroit Tigers future Baseball Hall of Fame center fielderTy Cobbmakes his MLB debut, doubling offJack Chesbroin a 5-3 win over the New York Highlanders at Bennett Park in Detroit
Joe Louisbeats Tommy Farr in 15 rounds for the heavyweight boxing title
Donald Bisset, British children book writer, illustrator (Upside Down Stories) and actor (Battle of the Sexes), born in Brentford, Middlesex, England (d. 1995)
William "Pop" Gates, American Basketball Hall of Fame guard and coach (Harlem Globetrotters "Legends" Ring 1995), born in Decatur, Alabama (d. 1999)
